Flatten Simulation Results into One Tidy Data Frame

Source: R/scenarios.R
tidy_simulation_results.Rd

Stack the $data of several saqr_sim objects into a single tidy data.frame. Each sim contributes its rows plus a .case column (the list name) and one column per scalar (length-1) ground-truth parameter in $params. Non-scalar parameters (matrices, vectors) are skipped. Columns are unioned across cases, so cases with different parameter sets still bind safely.

Usage

tidy_simulation_results(x)

Arguments

x

A named list of saqr_sim objects (for example the output of run_scenario), or a single saqr_sim object.

Value

A single base data.frame with all data rows stacked, a .case column, and one column per scalar parameter.

Examples

s <- run_scenario("power_ttest", seed = 1)
tidy_simulation_results(s)
#>     .case mean_a mean_b sd_a sd_b n_a n_b cohens_d group        score
#> 1   case1      0    0.2    1    1  30  30      0.2     A -0.896914547
#> 2   case1      0    0.2    1    1  30  30      0.2     A  0.184849185
#> 3   case1      0    0.2    1    1  30  30      0.2     A  1.587845331
#> 4   case1      0    0.2    1    1  30  30      0.2     A -1.130375674
